text: Medical requests should be submitted via med.swiss.com.

A travelling companion is required if the passenger is unable to help themselves during an emergency evacuation, unable to communicate with cabin crew regarding safety instructions, or unable to understand or follow safety instructions.
SWISS has joined the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower program, which supports people with invisible disabilities.

Wearing the sunflower symbol is a discreet way of indicating invisible disabilities, signaling to others that the passenger may need assistance or extra time. While the sunflower does not guarantee special services, SWISS staff will do their best to ensure the passenger travels in comfort.
For flights starting or ending in the USA, a copy of the precise wording of the applicable regulations is available upon request at all airports from which SWISS flies to the USA.

Forms can be obtained from the US Department of Transportation through the following channels: 
- Free hotline for passengers with disabilities: +1-800-778-4838 (voice) or +1-800-455-9880 (TTY)
- Aviation Consumer Protection Division: +1 202-366-2220 (voice) or +1 202-366-0511 (TTY)
- In writing: Air Consumer Protection Division, C-75, U.S. Department of Transportation, 1200 New Jersey Ave., SE., West Building, Room W96-432, Washington, DC 20590, USA.

Passengers wishing to bring an assistance animal on flights with a scheduled flight time of eight hours or more must notify the airline at least 48 hours in advance. Certified assistance dogs are carried in the cabin free of charge, and an airport escort service is available upon request.

Passengers must verify vaccination rules and entry restrictions for their destination country, as some countries impose strict requirements for animals, including assistance dogs, and may refuse entry without the necessary documentation or certificates.

A Medical Care Form (MEDIF) may be required depending on the severity of a disability. A MEDIF is mandatory in cases of acute disease or accident, if a stretcher or oxygen is required, or if a passenger requires a traveling companion for health reasons. Note that cabin crew can only provide limited assistance.

The MEDIF form can be requested at the time of booking or downloaded as a PDF. Completed forms must be uploaded to med.swiss.com no later than 48 hours before departure, or 72 hours before departure if a stretcher is required.

section: Group Travel for Passengers with Disabilities
text: Passengers with disabilities traveling as part of a group should complete the designated group form (PDF).
Passengers with a chronic but stable illness who travel frequently may be issued a Frequent Travellers Medical Card (FREMEC), which removes the requirement to complete a MEDIF form for every trip.

To ensure mobility devices fit through the cargo door, the following aircraft dimensions apply:
- A220-100: Width 118 cm (46.5 in), Height 83 cm (32.6 in)
- A220-300: Width 118 cm (46.5 in), Height 83 cm (32.7 in)
- A320/321: Width 182 cm (71.6 in), Height 124 cm (48.8 in)
- A330-343: Width 270 cm (106.3 in), Height 168 cm (66.1 in)
- A340-313: Width 270 cm (106.3 in), Height 168 cm (66.1 in)
- B777-300: Width 246 cm (96.9 in), Height 170 cm (66.9 in)
- Embraer E290/E295: Width 118 cm (46.5 in), Height 90 cm (35.4 in)
- Embraer E90/E95: Width 114 cm (44.8 in), Height 78-88 cm (30.7-34.6 in)

Weight restrictions for mobility devices are as follows:
- Embraer fleet: 80 kg
- A220-100 and A220-300: 149 kg
Passengers with restricted mobility should book their flights in good time and notify the airline of any special requirements.
Passengers with restricted mobility are requested to contact the Service Centre to make bookings and advance seat reservations.
Because long flights are physically demanding, passengers with health problems should obtain evidence of their fitness to fly.

Passengers who require an additional seat for physical reasons or for extra room can book one on all SWISS flights by contacting the Service Centre. Booking in advance helps avoid being denied carriage if the flight is fully booked.

The Department of Transportation is located at 1200 New Jersey Ave., SE., West Building, Room W96-432, Washington, DC 20590, USA. Additional information is available via the Aviation Consumer Protection Division website; please note that external websites may have different privacy, security, or accessibility policies.
SWISS is committed to handling wheelchairs and scooters (referred to as mobility devices) with care, although these devices may occasionally be damaged or delayed.

To report a delayed or damaged mobility device during a trip, passengers should file a report online via the SWISS 'Create a New Report' portal or contact the lost and found office at their destination airport.
Passengers can report delayed or damaged mobility devices by filing a report online through SWISS or by contacting the lost and found at the destination airport.
Passengers may request a reimbursement of expenses related to baggage problems by completing the SWISS application form for reimbursement of costs.

In the event of mobility device mishandling, passengers may request assistance from a Complaint Resolution Official (CRO). CROs are trained in US authority regulations and are available at all US destinations, as well as in Zurich and Geneva, Switzerland.
Complaint Resolution Officials (CROs) are available at all US destinations and in Switzerland at the Zurich and Geneva airports.
If a mobility device is delayed, passengers can choose to either pick up the device at the destination airport or have it delivered to a reasonable location, such as a home or hotel.

SWISS will reimburse transportation expenses to and from the airport incurred as a direct result of a delayed mobility device. Passengers may be required to submit documentation to substantiate these costs.

Swiss International Air Lines provides assistance to passengers with disabilities at the airport and during flights. Passengers are advised to consult their physician regarding trip preparations and to book their flight at least two days before departure, notifying the Service Centre of their specific requirements.

Passengers should book flights at least two days before departure and notify the Service Centre of their needs. Groups traveling with their own mobility aids are requested to notify the airline of their care requirements as early as possible.

Effective October 1, 2025, wheelchairs or other mobility aids equipped with permanently installed, non-removable lithium batteries will be limited to a maximum of two batteries, each with a capacity of up to 160 Wh.

The new lithium battery limits for mobility aids will not apply to passengers whose booking request was confirmed or whose trip commenced before October 1, 2025; such equipment will still be transported.

Specially trained staff are available to assist passengers with disabilities at all airports. Complimentary wheelchairs and accompanied transport vehicles are provided for passengers during both departure and arrival.

Upon arrival at the destination airport, a passenger's personal wheelchair will be returned either immediately or at the baggage carousel. If the wheelchair is returned at the carousel, the passenger will be transported there via an airport wheelchair.
Wheelchairs cannot be returned immediately after disembarkation at the following airports: Catania, Hanover, Nuremberg, Olbia, Palermo, Rotterdam, Stuttgart, and Valencia.
Passengers with disabilities are recommended to arrive at the check-in desk 90 minutes before the latest check-in time.
Passengers with disabilities receive priority boarding. The airline will carry up to two wheelchairs and assistance dogs free of charge.
Special services are not available in all languages.
If a passenger's preferred language is not offered, further communication regarding their application for special services will be conducted in English.
Onboard wheelchairs, designed to fit along the aircraft aisle to enable movement inside the cabin, are provided on all long-haul flights and on short-haul flights upon request.
Cabin crew provide assistance with stowing hand baggage, transporting wheelchair users to and from the lavatory, and unpacking or identifying food on meal trays.
Cabin crew are unable to provide medical services, direct assistance with eating and drinking, or assistance with using the toilet.
Seats on all aircraft are equipped with removable armrests.
Currently, 5 out of 12 Airbus A330-300 aircraft are equipped with wheelchair-accessible toilets.
For safety reasons, passengers with disabilities cannot be assigned seats in the emergency exit rows.
An airport escort service is available upon request for passengers with impaired hearing or vision. Certified assistance dogs are permitted in the cabin free of charge.
Passengers may be required to submit documentation to substantiate costs for reimbursement.

If a mobility device is mishandled during a flight, passengers have the right to receive a loaner wheelchair from SWISS. The airline will ensure the loaner meets the passenger's functional and safety requirements as closely as possible while they await the return, repair, or replacement of their own device.
If SWISS cannot provide a suitable loaner wheelchair, passengers may secure their own alternative loaner mobility device.
Costs associated with securing an alternative loaner mobility device will be reimbursed within 30 days of the submission of substantiating documentation.
In the event of a damaged or lost mobility device, SWISS will offer to handle the repair or replacement with a device of comparable function and safety at the airline's expense.

Passengers may arrange for the repair or replacement of their device through a preferred vendor. In this case, SWISS is responsible for transporting the device to the vendor and paying the vendor directly for the associated costs.
To ensure a mobility device fits through the cargo door, passengers should check the specific door sizes of the aircraft. SWISS will make every effort to accommodate the mobility device.
